title: Market Brief — Friday, June 19, 2026
summary: GPU supply is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 53% for H200, 38% for B200, 35% for A100, and 24% for H100, with every tier seeing 20-50pp utilization drops over 30 days. H100 spot fell 8.3% overnight to $2.20/hr, the sharpest single-day move, while H200 annualized volatility hit 158% signaling a market repricing a sudden demand vacuum.
paragraphs: GPU supply is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 53% for H200, 38% for B200, 35% for A100, and 24% for H100, with every tier seeing 20-50pp utilization drops over 30 days. H100 spot fell 8.3% overnight to $2.20/hr, the sharpest single-day move, while H200 annualized volatility hit 158% signaling a market repricing a sudden demand vacuum.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), B200 inference runs $4.00/M tokens for Llama 70B, making it the only GPU where mid-tier pricing breaks even. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling far below any hardware TCO floor, burning cash to hold share. Grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Strong, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

H200 compute is overvalued at $3.28/hr spot given 53% util-avail and cratering demand; short H200 capacity contracts and rotate into B200 at $4.38/hr where 71% of NeoCloud offerings sit free, signaling imminent price collapse that will make it the best margin GPU within 60 days.
bullets: 53% of H200 compute capacity is available by Ornn utilization, crossing into oversupply territory.
24% util-avail on H100 makes it the tightest GPU class but still eased 19.8pp in 30 days.
$2.20/hr H100 spot dropped 8.3% day-over-day, the largest single-session decline across all classes.
21.78 on the LLMflation index means frontier output tokens cost 78% less than GPT-4 launch pricing.
$0.18/M output for OpenAI gpt-oss-120b undercuts every open-source hosted alternative by roughly 40%.
158% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is 2.6x that of B200, flagging dangerous repricing risk for locked-in buyers.
